jvm-null-completion-stage.semantics: A custom Transport returning a null CompletionStage is one sanitized nonretryable IO/UNKNOWN/CUSTOM_TRANSPORT seam defect with no retry.

jvm-null-response-completion.semantics: A non-null CompletionStage completing with null response is the shared no-response defect with exactly one transport invocation.

jvm-response-factory-sync-throw.semantics: TransportResponse.of rejects invalid construction synchronously and closes a transferred non-null body exactly once before the throw escapes execute.

jvm-response-factory-failed-stage.semantics: Asynchronous response construction failure is a failed stage; the transferred body is already closed exactly once and core never acquires a response.

jvm-operation-executor-rejected.semantics: Operation submission rejection releases admission and settles OVERLOADED/NOT_SENT through the bounded terminal pool.

jvm-deadline-scheduler-rejected.semantics: Initial deadline registration rejection prevents operation work and settles OVERLOADED/NOT_SENT through the bounded terminal pool.

jvm-retry-scheduler-rejected.semantics: Retry timer rejection preserves unresolved commitment and settles OVERLOADED/POSSIBLY_SENT without a second attempt.

jvm-inline-executor-detected.semantics: A borrowed executor running inline before submit returns performs no user or network work and terminalizes CONFIGURATION/NOT_SENT on the terminal pool.

jvm-cancel-queued-operation.semantics: Native Future cancellation removes the exact queued task when possible, prevents ghost work, and publishes the stable outcome once.

jvm-close-queued-operation.semantics: Runtime close cancels queued work by identity, uses structured CLOSED, and preserves borrowed executor ownership.

jvm-repost-failure-reason-abi.semantics: RepostFailureReason exposes only the closed shared enum/remediation mapping with JSpecify nullness and no platform exception strings.

jvm-telemetry-live-context.semantics: Capture occurs at admission; operation/attempt scopes make the captured parent current around transport and owned continuations and every started handle ends once before publication.

jvm-telemetry-nonfatal-capture.semantics: A nonfatal capture failure disables telemetry for the operation, increments telemetryFailures once, and cannot change sending.

jvm-telemetry-nonfatal-start.semantics: A nonfatal/null start disables telemetry, increments telemetryFailures once, and cannot change sending.

jvm-telemetry-nonfatal-scope.semantics: A nonfatal/null makeCurrent failure is isolated; retained handles receive best-effort end exactly once.

jvm-telemetry-nonfatal-attempt.semantics: A nonfatal attempt start/end failure is isolated with no retry recursion and operation end still executes once.

jvm-telemetry-nonfatal-end.semantics: A nonfatal end failure is counted once after the end attempt and cannot replace the primary terminal result.

jvm-fatal-assertion-error.semantics: A non-VM AssertionError receives allocation-minimal deterministic cleanup, completes primary and outcome exceptionally with the same instance, releases settlement in finally, and reaches an owned-thread uncaught handler as the same instance without retry or Repost normalization.

jvm-fatal-linkage-error.semantics: A non-VM LinkageError from a borrowed/JDK completion is handed off without user code, deterministically settles primary and outcome with the same instance and balanced resources, then is rethrown as that same instance on an owned terminal thread.

jvm-close-aggregate-order.semantics: Nonfatal close failures aggregate in scheduler, transport, DNS, proxy credential, TLS, operation, terminal settlement, observer order while runtime still reaches CLOSED.

jvm-engine-source-vendor-lock.semantics: Verify every pinned upstream source archive, ordered minimal patch, patched tree, relocation, resource/service inventory, license, SBOM, provenance, and zero public or transitive upstream type.

jvm-engine-public-transport-options.semantics: Compile and execute the immutable HttpTransportOptions, proxy, DNS, TLS, borrowed ownership, mutual-exclusion, and non-overridable BUILTIN JSSE hostname-verification contract on JVM and native image.

jvm-engine-custom-transport-ordered-head.semantics: Compile and execute the public ordered HeaderField-list ABI and prove its bounded one-pass iterator snapshots only a valid prefix, preserves valid duplicate order and case, never traverses an amplification tail, and prevents shuffled Map sources from creating implicit ordering.

jvm-engine-custom-inputstream-close-live.semantics: Use latch-driven custom InputStreams with a pending read, cross-thread close, deadline, cancellation, runtime close, and saturated operation workers to prove close is prompt/callback-free, pending and future reads terminate, public terminal settlement progresses, and a later probe succeeds; separately bound the residual of an explicitly violating stream.

jvm-engine-shared-budget-reservations.semantics: Prove exact connection and stream reservation acquisition/release boundaries, separate immutable body-chunk charging, diagnostics inclusion, and nonblocking overload without publication.

jvm-engine-capacity-aware-pool.semantics: Drive cold bursts, ALPN single-flight, H1/H2 scale-out, peer stream limits 0/1/2/100/huge, FIFO cancellation, TTL, idle expiry, and GOAWAY replacement through the patched capacity-aware pool.

jvm-engine-h2-stream-local-terminalizati.semantics: Cancel a queued stream before publication and terminalize active cancel, peer reset, parser, response-cap, and decompression failures while same-session peers and later reuse succeed.

jvm-engine-h2-rst-delivery-retry.semantics: Drive REFUSED_STREAM, CANCEL, and stream PROTOCOL_ERROR before and after commitment through success and exhaustion while raw-counting exact attempts, header/body publications, unresolved attempts, stable-key reuse, peer survival, and later same-session probe.

jvm-engine-fifo-establishment-sponsor.semantics: Queue N operations behind one cold establishment and prove only the oldest sponsor consumes a DNS/TCP/TLS failure, cancellation transfers useful establishment to the next waiter, zero waiters cancels it, and no failure fans out or creates publications.

jvm-engine-bounded-happy-eyeballs.semantics: Drive ordered dual-stack addresses through at most two complete raced TCP/CONNECT/TLS/ALPN pipelines for IPv6-blackhole to IPv4 success, first-TCP TLS stall or bad certificate with alternate healthy, reverse-family success, both fail, cancel, close, direct and proxy paths, shared deadlines, exact candidate slices, and loser cleanup.

jvm-engine-core-and-reactor-timer-bounds.semantics: Drive connect/attempt ties, connection readiness, retry backoff, cancellation, candidate cadence, expiry sweeps, and close to prove exact 2N core task reuse and a separate threadless C+1 reactor wheel.

jvm-engine-h2-goaway-classification.semantics: Prove masked GOAWAY last-stream handling for local same-side streams, <=last retention, >last request-not-executed replay, queued-command cancellation, repeated GOAWAY, high reserved bit, peer isolation, and exact-once release.

jvm-engine-h2-unsigned-settings.semantics: Apply dynamic peer settings for zero-to-positive concurrency/window, reductions below active, unsigned huge values, and invalid frames without treating valid settings as connection-fatal.

jvm-engine-hpack-secret-bounds.semantics: Inspect raw HPACK while a huge peer table and rotating secret/idempotency/correlation/trace values prove secrets are NEVER_INDEXED, volatile values are WITHOUT_INDEXING, sensitivity survives relocation, and retained encoder bytes stay capped.

jvm-engine-bounded-outbound-request.semantics: Exercise exact and plus-one raw/canonical base URI, resolved target, H1 request-line, local H1/H2 field-list, H2 encoded-block, and dynamic peer SETTINGS_MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE boundaries before stream or body publication.

jvm-engine-exact-outbound-framing.semantics: Raw-capture zero, nonzero, and exact-maximum bodies on H1 and H2 and prove canonical actual-byte Content-Length, exact request/pseudo/regular field order and cardinality, explicit default-port authority preservation, local-cap accounting of automatic fields, and complete absence of Transfer-Encoding, chunked, duplicate Host/Content-Length, and connection-specific fields.

jvm-engine-combined-response-metadata-bu.semantics: Exercise H1 and H2 eight-versus-nine informational responses and combined informational/final/trailer wire, logical, and field limits with H2 peer survival.

jvm-engine-strict-framing.semantics: Reject H1 obs-fold, leading-name whitespace, TE plus CL, duplicate or invalid CL, unsupported/multiple TE, malformed chunks/extensions/trailers, and ambiguous framing with no connection reuse; reject forbidden H2 headers at stream scope.

jvm-engine-strict-utf8-report.semantics: Run H1/H2 multibyte boundaries plus malformed, overlong, truncated, control, DEL, and non-scalar header input through one REPORT-mode UTF-8 coding configuration and exact wire/logical accounting.

jvm-engine-bounded-dns-isolation.semantics: Block and saturate default and borrowed DNS resolution to prove caller/reactor isolation, deadline-before-submit, fixed-key bounded cache, exact rejection/removal, lifecycle ownership, and documented bounded noncooperative residual.

jvm-engine-dns-routing.semantics: Use split-horizon fixture names to prove direct mode resolves only the origin, CONNECT mode resolves only the proxy, preserves canonical origin CONNECT authority and TLS SNI, and applies BUILTIN origin hostname verification without any origin-resolver call.

jvm-engine-bounded-tls-isolation.semantics: Block engine creation, configuration, session sizing, begin/status, wrap, unwrap, PKIX, OCSP, FIPS, HSM, verifier, and close calls to prove the full async TLS adapter preserves reactor and unrelated-session progress, deadline settlement, bounded residuals, per-connection serialization, and bounded performance cost.

jvm-engine-tls-context-ownership.semantics: Prove synchronous construction only validates syntax/allowlists, then first establishment lazily creates/probes each default non-global TLS context on the TLS lane under deadlines without ambient client keys; isolate trust/sessions/global-default mutation, invalidate owned sessions after connection close, and never mutate a borrowed context.

jvm-engine-bounded-proxy-credential-isol.semantics: Block, cancel, fail, null, and saturate proxy credential providers to prove exact once-per-logical-establishment invocation on the credential pool, stable failure mapping, bounded candidate reuse, late-return discard, and wiping of every SDK-owned credential/CONNECT copy after all candidates settle.

jvm-engine-proxy-credential-validation.semantics: Run exact username/password boundary and invalid matrices, raw-observe the preemptive Basic CONNECT field, rotate credentials across logical establishments, and prove invalid/null values publish zero CONNECT and every SDK-owned representation is wiped.

jvm-engine-hardened-proxy-connect.semantics: Drive raw proxy CONNECT responses through cumulative informational/header caps, strict non-2xx framing/body limits, slow-body deadline, cancellation, close, status tagging, credential removal, and poisoned-connection non-reuse; prove final 2xx immediately becomes a tunnel and preserves coalesced origin TLS octets despite bogus Content-Length/Transfer-Encoding.

jvm-engine-budgeted-response-consumer.semantics: Exercise exact and plus-one bodies and cancellation at every chunk boundary to prove bounded reactor copying, immutable chunks, no duplicate body, completion-after-raw-end, nonblocking InputStream, exact-once release, and protocol-local abort.

jvm-engine-plaintext-zeroization.semantics: Inspect patched H1, H2, TLS, request-workspace, and raw/decompressed chunk storage after credential rotation, idle pool reuse, terminal cleanup, resize, and close; prove consumed/vacated SDK-owned plaintext is wiped while caller storage is untouched and wipe overhead stays bounded.

jvm-engine-jackson-nonrecycling-lifecycl.semantics: Construct every SDK JsonFactory with Jackson Core 2.21.2 nonRecyclingPool, disabled field-name canonicalization/interning, and exact stream constraints; parse unique unknown remote keys, then reuse one borrowed executor across isolated application-classloader redeploys and prove no recycler, symbol, key/payload sentinel, generated class, or application loader is retained.

jvm-engine-single-monotonic-close-budget.semantics: Nearly exhaust the runtime close budget, jump wall time, and self-close from reactor, operation, native-cancel, terminal, and observer identities while proving one injected-monotonic five-second bound and interrupt preservation.

jvm-engine-provider-worker-reentrant-clo.semantics: Invoke runtime.close reentrantly from DNS resolver, proxy credential provider, and custom JSSE provider callbacks while the current frame owns counted state; prove close returns honestly in CLOSING, skips only current, closes peers, preserves interrupt state, then current finally wipes/releases and completes the one close stage as CLOSED+zero; separately prove a nonreturning callback remains bounded and visibly CLOSING.

jvm-engine-disabled-ambient-paths.semantics: Prove the built-in engine ignores system properties and ambient proxy and disables upgrade, expect-continue, priority, redirects, retries, cookies, codecs, auth cache, target auth, server push, and hidden request replay.

jvm-engine-authoritative-otel.semantics: Prove the Repost OTel bridge is authoritative across operation, attempts, reactor, DNS, TLS, retry, CompletionStage, and Kotlin coroutines while relocated internals remain agent-invisible and the only optional wire propagation is a fresh bridge-generated W3C traceparent per attempt.

jvmFixtureProtocol.fatalErrorPolicy: For every Error except VirtualMachineError and ThreadDeath, allocation-minimal deterministic cleanup atomically releases public bytes/in-flight, completes primary and outcome exceptionally with the exact same Error instance outside Repost normalization/redaction, releases settlement in finally, then hands off and rethrows that same instance on an owned uncaught boundary; never retry or deliberately kill a borrowed/JDK/event-loop thread. VirtualMachineError and ThreadDeath receive allocation-minimal best-effort cleanup only and make no settlement guarantee.
jvmFixtureProtocol.telemetryProtocol: captureContext is the only admission/caller-thread hook; start/makeCurrent/attempt/end run on owned execution with captured parent, all scopes close in finally, every started handle ends once before public publication, nonfatal failures are count-only, and fatal Error follows fatalErrorPolicy.
jvmFixtureProtocol.oneShotPublicationProtocol: One fresh internal async entity producer per core attempt; transport retry and authentication replay are disabled, queued work has no producer assignment, and wireBodyPublications must be <= coreAttemptCount under stale H1, proxy 407, reset, RST, SETTINGS, and GOAWAY stimuli.
jvmFixtureProtocol.failureReasonProtocol: The JVM public type is RepostFailureReason with the exact shared enum/remediation mapping; no public exception class, package, host, certificate, proxy, or remote string is retained.
jvmFixtureProtocol.resourceOwnershipProtocol: The first close linearizes OPEN to CLOSING and creates one 5000ms monotonic deadline; exact owned close order is scheduler, relocated transport pool/connections/reactor including EngineTimerWheel, DNS executor, proxy credential executor, TLS executor with owned session invalidation before shutdown, operation executor, terminal settlement pool, observer dispatcher; injected SSLContext, resolver, proxy credential provider, and custom Transport are borrowed; reentrant close excludes only its current identity and may return CLOSING with closed=false and bytes still counted; current finally wipes/releases and the coordinator publishes CLOSED only at inFlight=0,bufferedBytes=0 before settling the single immutable closeCompletion stage; later close waits only the original remainder and a nonreturning current callback leaves an honest bounded-residual CLOSING state.

jvmRawFixtureProtocol.transport: HTTP/1.1 JSON controls over the exact loopback controlEndpoint from the one-line handshake; no authentication token, query, fragment, redirect, ambient proxy, or non-loopback address is permitted

handshakeSchema.name: jvm-raw-fixture-handshake-v1
handshakeSchema.rules: one scalar-valid UTF-8 JSON line with exactly the four fields; protocolVersion=1; groupId equals the requested group; controlEndpoint matches http://127.0.0.1:<1..65535>/control; pid is a positive safe integer; no secret or filesystem path is present

controlProtocol.rules: POST one scalar-valid UTF-8 JSON object to the exact controlEndpoint; reject unknown or duplicate fields, unknown actions, invalid order, barriers not declared by the selected group, and nonpositive or unsafe deltaMs; releaseBarrier releases only the named fixture stimulus, while the independently observed raw event path updates counters; every accepted action returns exactly one closed-schema response

scenarioSchema.rules: connectHost is exact 127.0.0.1 or ::1 and both families are mandatory for each Happy-Eyeballs group; port is 1..65535; authorityHost and basePath are bounded printable ASCII without credentials, query, fragment, control, or traversal; alpnProtocols is an ordered subset of h2,http/1.1; asset paths resolve only inside the fixture source tree or .superpowers/prototypes/2026-07-13-jvm-sdk-task-2-evidence and never appear in retained evidence

optionalFeaturePruning.policy: v1 compiles no upstream content-compression, optional codec, alternate TLS-provider, classic-client, or ambient authentication feature path; the exact sorted included-source inventory and excluded-source inventory are derived from the SHA-pinned materialized archives after patching and are themselves hash-pinned before seal
optionalFeaturePruning.contentCompression: disabled structurally; Repost owns exact identity/gzip response decoding outside Apache content-codec execution and sends only the frozen Accept-Encoding gzip field
optionalFeaturePruning.proof: compile the exact included manifest on --release 11 with only JDK plus the pinned relocated fork sources; reject prohibited imports, constant-pool strings, service entries, coordinates, classes, and transitive dependencies in source, classes, multi-release entries, POM, Gradle metadata, SBOM, and staged consumer graphs

verification.archiveNamespacePolicy: the built archive may contain relocated fork classes only below sh.repost.internal.apache; reject any class, service descriptor, multi-release entry, string-referenced implementation, or exported/public signature below sh.repost.internal.http, org.apache.hc, or org.slf4j, and reject original org.apache.hc or org.slf4j runtime dependencies

publicApi.baseUriConvenienceOverloads: none in v1; URI cannot preserve every raw lexical state required by the portable validator
publicApi.closeCompletionIdentity: return the same read-only immutable CompletionStage instance for the runtime lifetime; callers cannot complete, cancel, or replace the coordinator future
publicApi.customTransportConflict: Transport and HttpTransportOptions are mutually exclusive CONFIGURATION/NOT_SENT before snapshot or side effects
publicApi.transportHeaders: TransportHeaderField.of(name,value) captures any non-null raw immutable Java strings, including unpaired surrogate, control, CR/LF, and oversize values, without syntax/Unicode/limit validation; its toString is constant redacted and contains no name/value; TransportRequest.getHeaderFields(), TransportResponse.getHeaderFields(), and TransportResponse.of(status,List<TransportHeaderField>,body) use immutable ordered lists; the response factory transfers non-null body ownership at entry, rejects status outside 200..599 or a null list as structural factory failure and closes body once, then obtains and consumes the caller list iterator in one bounded pass: validate and copy each reached immutable field in order, retain only the valid prefix, stop touching the iterator immediately on null field, iterator acquisition/hasNext/next nonfatal failure, first HeaderFailureKind.PROTOCOL, or the 101st field HeaderFailureKind.LIMIT; null/iterator failures are structural factory failures that close body once and throw the SDK-owned factory exception with no remote values, while protocol/limit return an acquired known-status response whose body remains owned and whose ordered header list contains only the valid retained prefix; a valid response requires observed iterator exhaustion by at most 100 fields; the 101st field contributes only bounded failure diagnostics, is never retained, and no 102nd element or million-entry tail is reached; retain no caller list/iterator reference; exact valid duplicate and mixed-case interleaving is preserved; no Map-authoritative overload or unspecified HashMap iteration is part of the ABI
publicApi.customTransportBody: TransportResponse raw InputStream permits at most one reader; close from another thread is prompt, nonblocking, interruption-safe, and callback-free, unblocks a pending read, and makes every pending/future read promptly return EOF or one sanitized IOException; core tracks the acquired response and calls close on deadline, cancellation, and runtime close; a stream violating this public contract is unsupported, public terminal settlement still uses the bounded terminal lane, and at most Wop operation workers may remain residually blocked until violating reads return
publicApi.baseUri: validate and canonical-roundtrip the raw value before platform URI parsing: ASCII RFC3986 only, total <=2048 bytes, authority <=512 bytes, base path <=1536 bytes; path permits slash, unreserved, sub-delims, colon, at-sign, or canonical percent escapes with uppercase hex only; reject raw non-ASCII, malformed or lowercase percent escapes, encoded slash or backslash, dot or dot-dot segments, backslash, controls, space, query, and fragment; the resolved origin-form final path with no query is <=2048 bytes and the full canonical URI is <=4096 bytes

httpTransportOptions.ownership: all supplied objects are borrowed and never closed
httpTransportOptions.hostnameVerification: HostnameVerificationPolicy.BUILTIN with JSSE endpoint identification; not disableable or replaceable
httpTransportOptions.tlsProtocols: construction validates only that a caller list is a nonempty ordered subset of TLSv1.3,TLSv1.2; default and supported-provider intersection is probed lazily on the TLS lane during first physical establishment under remaining connect, attempt, and operation deadlines
httpTransportOptions.tlsCipherSuites: construction validates only that a caller list is a nonempty subset of the frozen modern allowlist; default and supported-provider intersection is probed lazily on the TLS lane during first establishment under deadlines; use custom Transport for legacy or weaker suites
httpTransportOptions.customSslContext: may change trust policy but cannot weaken or replace BUILTIN hostname verification; it is borrowed and its configuration, session cache, sessions, and lifetime are never mutated or invalidated

publicApi.dnsResolver: synchronous thread-safe borrowed List<InetAddress> resolve(String asciiHost); invoked only on the owned DNS pool
publicApi.dnsRouting: direct connections resolve only the canonical origin host; HTTP CONNECT connections resolve only the proxy host, invoke the origin resolver zero times, send the canonical origin authority in CONNECT, and still use the canonical origin for TLS SNI and non-overridable BUILTIN hostname verification

httpProxyOptions.uriConvenienceOverloads: none in v1; raw lexical validation occurs before internal URI construction
httpProxyOptions.uri: validate the raw String before platform URI construction; require exact http:// ASCII host plus explicit port; reject userinfo, query, fragment, non-root path, missing port, HTTPS proxy, non-ASCII host, malformed/lowercase escapes, backslash, controls, and noncanonical roundtrip
httpProxyOptions.originRestriction: proxying is valid only for HTTPS origins
httpProxyOptions.usernameValidation: 1..256 visible US-ASCII octets 0x21..0x7e excluding colon; reject empty, SP, control, DEL, non-ASCII, and colon
httpProxyOptions.passwordValidation: 1..4096 printable US-ASCII octets 0x20..0x7e including colon, with at least one non-SP octet; reject empty, all-SP, control, DEL, and non-ASCII
httpProxyOptions.credentialInvocation: exactly once per logical origin-connection establishment on the owned credential-isolation pool before the first CONNECT candidate; never on caller, operation, reactor, DNS, or TLS threads; copy once for bounded candidate failover, retain only until every candidate settles, and leave the provider value caller-owned
httpProxyOptions.credentialFailures: invalid value, cancel, exception, or null is CONFIGURATION/PROXY_CREDENTIAL_PROVIDER with zero CONNECT; credential-pool saturation is OVERLOADED; late returns are discarded
httpProxyOptions.credentialWiping: wipe every SDK-owned copied username/password char, US-ASCII credential byte, Base64 byte, Proxy-Authorization header buffer, auth object, and CONNECT object on every path; never mutate the PasswordAuthentication returned by caller code
httpProxyOptions.authentication: preemptive Basic on CONNECT only; encode exact US-ASCII username:password then RFC4648 Base64 with normal padding and no wrapping; 407 is terminal PROXY/NOT_SENT; no challenge replay, NTLM, Kerberos, PAC, or system proxy

byteBudget.bootstrap: owned built-in runtime atomically reserves one 1048576-byte HTTP_BOOTSTRAP_ESTABLISHMENT reservation at construction, split into two exact 524288 candidate slices; during one logical establishment each slice may back one complete TCP plus CONNECT-if-configured plus TLS plus ALPN pipeline; after the first fully origin-ready winner, wipe/release loser resources, retain one 524288 winner slice with the physical connection, and retain the other 524288 as reconnect hedge; the reservation transfers without extra acquisition or double-counting and remains until runtime close
byteBudget.additionalConnections: each extra ready-or-establishing pool entry opportunistically reserves 1048576 free bytes for its two candidate slices; failure reduces scale-out and queues/reuses guaranteed capacity rather than rejecting work; after readiness release the loser 524288 slice and retain the winner slice until physical destruction; no extra establishment begins unless the full 1048576 is available
byteBudget.operationAcquireBefore: atomically reserve the entire operation amount before every SDK snapshot, provider/default/key hook, copy, serialization, DNS, or network action; if reservation fails while idle non-bootstrap connections hold permits, issue one O(1) pool/reactor command that marks the oldest such entries for asynchronous immediate close, but still reject the current operation; never synchronously tear down an Apache session on the public send path; active extras remain charged and every connection permit releases only after actual buffer, command, and session destruction
byteBudget.operationRejection: the current operation is OVERLOADED/NOT_SENT, releases admission, and performs zero observer/provider/default/key/serialization/DNS/network/request-publication work; after asynchronous idle-extra destruction releases permits, a later independent send may succeed through the bootstrap connection
byteBudget.requestWorkspace: serialize within 1048576 bytes, convert only actual retained request bytes at completion, release unused workspace, and retain actual request bytes across retries
byteBudget.responseAndStreamLifetime: hold the full 1048576 response workspace, distinct 262144 JSON parser scratch reservation, and 262144 stream reservation across all retries until terminal cleanup and confirmed final HttpCore stream/exchange removal; clear/reuse response and parser workspaces between attempts; the next retry cannot reuse stream capacity until prior removal is confirmed
byteBudget.responseStreaming: stream raw to decoded using immutable chunks of at most 16384 bytes by transfer/reuse within response workspace; identity transfer is zero-copy; at most 16384 transient scratch bytes are charged inside stream reservation; no post-publication byte acquisition
byteBudget.customTransport: atomically reserve both 1048576 workspaces plus the distinct 262144 JSON parser scratch reservation, total 2359296, before hooks and side effects, but no bootstrap or stream reservation
byteBudget.diagnostics: RuntimeDiagnostics.bufferedBytes includes every workspace/reservation; an idle open built-in runtime reports exactly 1048576 before the first establishment, exactly 1048576 while two candidates race, exactly 1048576 after winner transfer plus reconnect hedge, and zero after runtime close
byteBudget.progress: at the 4194304 minimum, one built-in operation reserves exactly 2621440 and HTTP_BOOTSTRAP_ESTABLISHMENT reserves 1048576, total 3670016 with exactly 524288 headroom; cold near-max operations and an exact max request plus max raw or near-1MiB incompressible-gzip response prove progress, no post-publication overload, exact release, and later probe

pool.physicalOriginConnectionCap: C=min(maxInFlightOperations,32) ready-or-establishing pool entries; transient TCP/CONNECT/TLS/ALPN candidate sockets are separately bounded P<=2C and never become independent pool entries
pool.h2SessionCap: H=min(C,4), counting only eligible plus connecting H2 sessions; draining H2 sessions do not count toward H but still count toward C and retain byte reservations
pool.queue: one global FIFO ordered by attempt-enqueue sequence; cancellation removes the exact entry
pool.coldStart: the oldest FIFO entry sponsors one cold/new physical establishment until ALPN is known; DNS/TCP/TLS failure consumes only the sponsor's core transport attempt and never fans out to waiters; sponsor cancellation transfers a still-useful establishment to the next waiter and cancels establishment only when no waiter remains; after failure the next FIFO entry may sponsor
pool.h1ScaleOut: open only demand-needed physical connections up to C
pool.h2Selection: eligible session with lowest active/effective load; ties use oldest connection ID
pool.h2ScaleOut: when all sessions are saturated, open exactly one session at a time on demand only when physical count<C, eligible-plus-connecting H2 count<H, and an extra connection reservation is available; otherwise queue globally
pool.publicationRule: do not assign an exchange or create a stream until capacity exists; queued cancellation publishes zero bytes
pool.peerLimitReduction: a limit below active drains naturally and accepts no new stream until below the effective cap
pool.draining: TTL expiry or GOAWAY makes the session ineligible, drains eligible existing streams, requeues only proven-unprocessed work, destroys the session, returns bootstrap only after destruction, and then replaces under demand; new attempts may queue under the existing deadline and no availability is promised if replacement, DNS, or TLS fails
pool.eligible: OPEN and ACTIVE, unexpired, not GOAWAY/draining, and below both SDK and peer stream caps
pool.sponsorFailure: N queued plus one connect failure or cancellation has exact establishment, failed-attempt, waiter, publication, and reservation counts; coalesced/negative DNS prevents retry storms
pool.happyEyeballs: preserve resolver order within each address family and interleave families; the first complete TCP plus CONNECT-if-configured plus origin TLS plus ALPN pipeline starts immediately, the first opposite-family complete pipeline starts at 250ms, then one next remaining complete pipeline may replace a settled loser each 250ms while capacity exists; race at most two complete readiness pipelines per logical pool establishment and globally at most 2C transient candidate sockets; a winner is the first candidate fully origin-ready after CONNECT, TLS, and ALPN, never merely first TCP; close and wipe every loser after a winner, while cancellation/close stops and wipes all candidates; the reactor-owned EngineTimerWheel supplies one reusable candidate-cadence slot per establishing pool entry and never consumes a core scheduler entry; the 1048576-byte establishment reservation supplies two exact 524288 candidate slices with no extra acquisition; invoke proxy credentials once per logical establishment, copy once for both pipelines, then wipe the SDK copy only after all candidates settle

http2.settings: parse unsigned RFC values; MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS=0 and INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE=0 are valid and queue without publication; huge unsigned concurrency/header-list values clamp to SDK caps; initial window accepts 0..2147483647; invalid frames are connection-fatal
http2.goaway: mask lastStreamId with 0x7fffffff; retain same-side local streams <=last; fail same-side local streams >last as request-not-executed, cancel queued commands, and release once; repeated GOAWAY is monotonic
http2.streamLocalFailures: caller cancel, peer RST_STREAM including REFUSED/CANCEL/stream PROTOCOL_ERROR, local cap/parser/decompression abort release only the logical stream lease and preserve the session, peers, and subsequent reuse

rstDeliveryAndRetry.refusedStream: REFUSED_STREAM is transport-proven unprocessed: the affected attempt is NOT_SENT and retry-eligible with the same stable idempotency key even when request header/body bytes were published; it contributes zero unresolved attempts
rstDeliveryAndRetry.cancel: peer CANCEL before the first request-body octet accepted by transport is IO with failure reason CONNECTION_RESET and NOT_SENT; after commitment it is IO/CONNECTION_RESET with unresolved POSSIBLY_SENT
rstDeliveryAndRetry.protocolError: peer stream PROTOCOL_ERROR before commitment is IO with failure reason UNKNOWN and NOT_SENT; after commitment it is IO/UNKNOWN with unresolved POSSIBLY_SENT
rstDeliveryAndRetry.retry: CANCEL and stream PROTOCOL_ERROR are retry-eligible within the normal core-attempt and operation-deadline budget because every POST reuses one stable idempotency key; exhaustion preserves the final unresolved POSSIBLY_SENT delivery when any committed attempt remains unresolved
rstDeliveryAndRetry.isolation: every case is stream-local; same-session peer streams and a subsequent probe survive, and lease/publication/unresolved counters release exactly once

http2.connectionFatalFailures: only connection-level protocol, TLS, or I/O failure makes the physical session nonreusable

http1.framing: reject obs-fold, leading field-name whitespace, TE+CL, duplicate or invalid Content-Length, unsupported or multiple Transfer-Encoding, invalid chunks/extensions/trailers, and ambiguous informational/final framing before body; abort and never reuse the connection

proxyConnect.request: plaintext proxy bytes are exactly CONNECT canonical-origin-authority HTTP/1.1, one Host with the same authority, and zero or one Proxy-Authorization plus only explicitly frozen fixed proxy fields; no origin Authorization, Idempotency-Key, traceparent, SDK semantic field, body, Content-Length, or Transfer-Encoding is present
proxyConnect.classification: tag every proxy-generated response before generic origin status classification; expose no proxy header or body value
proxyConnect.metadataAndFraming: apply cumulative predecode wire, logical UTF-8, field, line, syntax, obs-fold, smuggling, and maximum-8 informational-response caps through the final CONNECT response head; for a final non-2xx only, apply strict H1 Content-Length/Transfer-Encoding/chunk/trailer framing; for a final 2xx, RFC 9110 CONNECT tunnel semantics supersede message-body framing and Content-Length or Transfer-Encoding is ignored
proxyConnect.body: for a final non-2xx only, count and process-and-discard at most 1048576 raw body bytes through a transient parser window of at most 65536 bytes, retain zero proxy response bytes after consumption/cleanup, then close; no successful 2xx proxy response body exists
proxyConnect.sequencing: on final 2xx, enter tunnel state immediately after the header terminator and hand every already-read trailing octet from the same socket read to origin TLS before requesting more input; never discard, reinterpret, or wait on those octets as a proxy body; final non-2xx framing/discard, cancellation, and close remain under the same connect, attempt, and operation deadlines before any origin TLS or request publication
proxyConnect.outcomes: 407 maps exactly PROXY/NOT_SENT; every other non-success, malformed, oversize, timeout, cancellation, or close path uses its stable pre-origin typed result and never reuses the physical connection
proxyConnect.credentials: remove and wipe every SDK-owned AuthExchange, CONNECT object, Proxy-Authorization header byte, Basic token, and password copy on success, failure, timeout, cancellation, and close
proxyConnect.originSeparation: after tunnel establishment the origin request carries the exact origin framing and SDK semantic fields and contains zero Proxy-Authorization; raw fixture inspection treats plaintext CONNECT and tunneled origin bytes as two independent sentinel domains

combinedResponseMetadataBudget.scope: all informational responses plus final headers plus trailers for one exchange
combinedResponseMetadataBudget.overflow: the ninth informational response or first over-budget wire/logical octet/field aborts H1 connection or resets only the H2 stream
combinedResponseMetadataBudget.lifetime: initialize before the first response head and release only when the exchange/stream is removed

protocol.utf8: one explicit UTF-8 CharCodingConfig with CodingErrorAction.REPORT for every H1/H2 encoder and decoder; predecode uses wire octets and postdecode uses the shared logical UTF-8 formula; reject malformed, overlong, truncated, NUL/C0/DEL, and non-scalar input

outboundRequestLimits.computation: materialize and account the exact resolved request target and complete H1/H2 pseudo-plus-regular outbound field set before exchange assignment, H2 stream creation, HPACK encoding, or body publication
outboundRequestLimits.portableFraming: implement the shared outboundFramingRule exactly: canonical actual-byte Content-Length and never Transfer-Encoding/chunked; H1 POST origin-form HTTP/1.1 with Host, Content-Length, five SDK semantic fields, then optional traceparent; H2 exact :method,:scheme,:authority,:path order followed by Content-Length, the five semantic fields, then optional traceparent, with no regular Host or connection-specific field
outboundRequestLimits.headerList: at most 16 logical fields and 16384 RFC bytes using exact name-octets + value-octets + 32 per field; each name <=256 bytes and value <=8192 bytes
outboundRequestLimits.headerCapScope: count every H2 pseudo-field and every transport-generated Host, Content-Length, semantic, and optional traceparent field toward both the 16-field and 16384-byte local caps
outboundRequestLimits.h1RequestLine: origin-form request line is at most 2080 wire bytes
outboundRequestLimits.h2HeaderFrames: the encoded header block must fit one HEADERS plus at most two CONTINUATION frames under the 16384-byte frame cap
outboundRequestLimits.peerMaxHeaderList: honor peer SETTINGS_MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE including zero and dynamic changes; when the exact actual request list exceeds the current peer value, keep the attempt in global FIFO under its existing attempt and operation deadlines without stream creation, HPACK mutation, or body publication; resume after a sufficient increase; a decrease never aborts an existing stream
outboundRequestLimits.peerTimeout: ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT/NOT_SENT through the normal stable retry budget
outboundRequestLimits.failures: a structural base URI, path, API-key-derived header, user-agent suffix, or other public configuration breach is construction CONFIGURATION/NOT_SENT; an impossible post-validation SDK-owned local-cap invariant breach is sanitized nonretryable IO/NOT_SENT with cause category HTTP_RUNTIME

protocol.responseBody: BudgetedResponseConsumer performs only bounded copy/accounting on reactor threads, grants capacity only from fixed reservation or acquired weighted bytes, stores immutable <=16384-byte chunks, completes TransportResponse only after capped raw body completion, and returns an idempotent nonblocking chunk InputStream whose close releases once; no pipe or ByteArrayOutputStream

plaintextZeroization.scope: stock reusable H1 ExpandableBuffer regions, H2 frame and header-block storage, TLS plaintext buffers, request serialization workspace, and raw/decompressed response chunks
plaintextZeroization.rule: wipe every consumed or vacated byte region before clear, compact, release, resize, pool reuse, response-workspace reuse, or physical close; wipe request and response chunks on every terminal path; no stale plaintext from a prior credential or payload may remain in an idle pooled H1/H2 connection
plaintextZeroization.ownership: wipe only SDK-owned copies and storage; never mutate caller PasswordAuthentication arrays or custom Transport/provider storage
plaintextZeroization.limitation: Java String values, including API keys supplied or produced as String, cannot be reliably zeroized; this limitation is public and does not weaken zeroization of SDK-owned byte and char copies
plaintextZeroization.performance: the patched-tree benchmark must pin a bounded wipe-overhead threshold for exact and near-maximum H1/H2 request and response workloads

serialization.jacksonCore: 2.21.2 exactly for the supported runtime line; no jackson-databind dependency
serialization.jsonFactory: construct every SDK-owned JsonFactory explicitly with JsonRecyclerPools.nonRecyclingPool(), disable JsonFactory.Feature.CANONICALIZE_FIELD_NAMES and INTERN_FIELD_NAMES, and install the exact frozen stream read/write constraints; never use default ThreadLocal/SoftReference recyclers or persistent field-name symbols
serialization.parserScratch: exactly one distinct 262144-byte reservation is acquired atomically with request, response, and target transport reservations before side effects, retained across retries, reported in bufferedBytes, and released once at terminal cleanup; field-name, scalar-string, numeric-token, and fixed/prebounded duplicate-detection structures cannot grow beyond it; exact limits are accepted and the first +1 resource breach is nonretryable RESPONSE_TOO_LARGE/POSSIBLY_SENT for 2xx while malformed syntax remains RESPONSE_PROTOCOL; no parser scratch is hidden inside response-body storage or acquired after publication
serialization.responseParsing: stream successful responses against exactly successResponseFieldOrder from the shared response schema; unknown names remain operation-local and are never interned or canonicalized; duplicate detection uses one bounded operation-local structure that is wiped where storage permits and discarded at parser close
serialization.globalState: never read, mutate, or depend on Jackson global defaults or system properties to select recycler, constraints, factory, symbol tables, or behavior
serialization.lifecycle: after an operation releases its SDK-owned parser/generator/buffer references, every discarded Jackson buffer is immediately GC-eligible and no borrowed executor thread retains an SDK/Jackson recycler, payload sentinel, generated class, or application classloader
serialization.certification: run repeated isolated application-classloader redeploys through one borrowed long-lived executor and responses with unique/sentinel unknown remote keys, then close parser/runtime and perform weak-reference plus payload/key-sentinel heap/GC assertions across later operations; publish a pinned allocation/throughput/latency benchmark for non-recycling and non-canonicalizing factories versus the forbidden defaults

isolation.coreScheduler: for each admitted operation register exactly one operationDeadlineTask plus at most one reusable phaseTask, so queued/running delayed tasks are at most 2N; during physical establishment phaseTask is due at min(connect deadline, attempt deadline) and CONNECT_TIMEOUT wins an exact tie; immediately after connection readiness cancel and exact-remove that same phase task, then reuse its slot for the remaining attempt deadline; after a retryable attempt settles, cancel/remove and reuse the same phase slot for retry due, then establishment/attempt timing on the next core attempt; cancellation, terminal settlement, and close exact-remove both tasks and stale generations are no-ops
isolation.engineTimerWheel: one monotonic timer wheel owned and driven by the relocated transport reactor, with no thread and zero core-scheduler entries; capacity is exactly C reusable Happy-Eyeballs candidate-cadence slots plus exactly one global TTL/idle-expiry sweep slot, total C+1; each physical connection owns at most one candidate slot, the 5000ms sweep reuses its one slot, cancel/connection destruction removes candidate state, and TRANSPORT_CLOSE clears every slot before reactor/pool destruction
isolation.threads: lazy owned daemon threads with stable names, platform or null context classloader, exact-identity queued-task removal, and cancellation that severs every runtime callback/reference
isolation.dns: deadline authority registers before submission; coalesce exactly one in-flight lookup per ASCII host; fixed origin+proxy keys only; SDK outer-cache positive TTL 60s and UnknownHost-only negative TTL 10s begin at completion monotonic time; never cache timeout, cancellation, overload, or provider failures; at most 64 addresses per result in resolver order; disclose that JVM security DNS caches may be longer
isolation.tls: every potentially provider-controlled SSLContext, SSLEngine, SSLSessionContext, SSLSession, and owned SecureRandom call runs on the bounded TLS lane with per-connection serialization: context/random creation and init, engine creation/configuration/session sizing, beginHandshake/status, wrap, unwrap, every delegated task including PKIX/OCSP/FIPS/HSM, BUILTIN verifier calls, session-cache enumeration/lookup/invalidation, and closeInbound/closeOutbound; each connection has at most one active or queued TLS task; reactor threads only move fixed encrypted/plain buffers and post continuations; blocking wrap/unwrap/session/random/provider calls cannot block unrelated sessions or the reactor
isolation.tlsContextOwnership: the hardened default never reads, uses, or changes the global default SSLContext; synchronous runtime/options construction performs only syntax/allowlist validation and defensive copies and invokes zero provider-controlled TLS calls; after first send registers connect, attempt, and operation deadlines, first physical establishment lazily runs SSLContext.getInstance("TLS"), one init with empty KeyManager[0], default TrustManager selection, owned SecureRandom, supported protocol/cipher intersection, buffer-size validation, and engine configuration on the TLS lane, then caches the successful runtime-owned context/session context; unsupported selection, provider failure, or subreserve breach is nonretryable TLS/NOT_SENT with failure reason TLS_NEGOTIATION and cause category TLS_PROVIDER; timeout/cancel/close settles and discards every late provider result while leaving a closeable runtime under bounded noncooperative residual rules; runtime close first closes every connection, then uses the TLS lane under the single remaining five-second close budget to call SSLSessionContext.getIds/getSession and SSLSession.invalidate before TLS-lane shutdown and context release; blocked session-cache calls are bounded by Wtls residuals and late results are discarded; no ambient javax.net.ssl client key or later global-default mutation affects it; a supplied SSLContext is borrowed, never initialized, mutated, enumerated, or invalidated, and all permitted engine/probe calls are likewise deferred to the TLS lane under establishment deadlines
isolation.proxyCredentials: provider runs only on Wcred, exactly once per logical establishment before the first CONNECT candidate, with the same deadline, exact-removal, ownership, late-return discard, and saturation rules; one SDK copy may span bounded candidates and is wiped only after every candidate settles
isolation.noncooperativeProvider: Java cannot kill noncooperative native DNS, JSSE, borrowed resolver, SSLContext, or credential provider calls; deadline/cancel settles publicly but at most Wdns, Wtls, or Wcred daemon calls may outlive close; platform/null context classloader and callback severing do not guarantee classloader collection because a never-returning FutureTask/wrapper/provider may retain its application loader until return or process exit; zero-retention certification applies only to cooperative/default returning paths

builder.hostnameVerification: HostnameVerificationPolicy.BUILTIN plus JSSE endpoint identification, always
builder.originPublication: one core attempt produces at most one origin request-body publication

observability.authority: Repost OpenTelemetry bridge, never external agent recognition of relocated internals
observability.spans: exactly one INTERNAL operation span plus at most 10 INTERNAL per-attempt child spans; retry delay is an event on the applicable SDK span, never a separate span
observability.propagation: captured context crosses reactor, DNS, TLS, retry continuations, CompletionStage, and Kotlin coroutines
observability.wirePropagation: only the Repost bridge may generate one W3C traceparent value from the current per-attempt span; exact lowercase form 00-{32 nonzero lowercase hex trace-id}-{16 nonzero lowercase hex span-id}-{2 lowercase hex flags}, exactly 55 ASCII bytes; regenerate per retry attempt and encode WITHOUT_INDEXING; when the bridge is absent or context is invalid, omit it; never emit tracestate, baggage, or any ambient/global composite-propagator header
observability.requestHeaderCardinality: every production request has exactly five SDK semantic fields Authorization, Content-Type, Accept-Encoding, User-Agent, and Idempotency-Key plus zero or one bridge-generated traceparent; protocol Host, Content-Length, and H2 pseudo-fields are accounted separately by outbound framing
observability.customTransportSpans: a custom Transport may create lower-level CLIENT spans as children of the SDK INTERNAL attempt span; the public Transport API remains execute-only and exposes no instrumentation declaration or suppression seam
observability.forbidden: relocated internals create no agent-visible span and no span contains authorization, credentials, bodies, arbitrary endpoint URLs, schema payload data, or unbounded attributes

fatalErrors.deterministic: for every Error except VirtualMachineError and ThreadDeath, perform allocation-minimal deterministic cleanup, atomically release public bytes and in-flight admission, complete both primary and outcome exceptionally with the exact same Error instance outside Repost normalization/redaction, release the settlement reservation in finally, and only then hand off and rethrow the same instance on an owned uncaught boundary; never retry or deliberately terminate a borrowed/JDK/event-loop thread
fatalErrors.unrecoverable: VirtualMachineError and ThreadDeath receive allocation-minimal best-effort cleanup only; no settlement, allocation, observer, or close guarantee is made after the unrecoverable throwable
fatalErrors.proof: AssertionError and LinkageError vectors require exact instance identity on primary, outcome, owned uncaught rethrow, balanced in-flight/bytes/settlement counters, later probe progress, and bounded close

close.rule: the first close linearizes OPEN to CLOSING, creates the one original 5000ms monotonic deadline, rejects later sends, and consumes only that deadline's remainder; stop new leases/streams, then close and aggregate in exact SCHEDULER_CLOSE, TRANSPORT_CLOSE, DNS_EXECUTOR_CLOSE, PROXY_CREDENTIAL_EXECUTOR_CLOSE, TLS_EXECUTOR_CLOSE, OPERATION_EXECUTOR_CLOSE, TERMINAL_SETTLEMENT_CLOSE, OBSERVER_CLOSE order; SCHEDULER_CLOSE exact-removes both per-operation core task slots, TRANSPORT_CLOSE clears the C+1 EngineTimerWheel and closes pool/connections/reactor, TLS_EXECUTOR_CLOSE submits owned session enumeration/invalidation on the still-live TLS lane before shutdown using only the remainder; attempt all eight stages, emit at most one ordered category per failed stage, cancel or drain within the remainder, use immediate pool/reactor close and bounded joins, and grant no nested grace period; publish CLOSED only in one coordinator epoch after inFlightOperations and bufferedBytes are both zero and every cooperative owned stage terminates, then complete the single closeCompletion stage normally or exceptionally with the one ordered aggregate close failure; later external close calls wait only the original remaining budget on that same completion
close.selfClose: reactor, operation, terminal, native-cancel, observer, DNS-provider, proxy-credential-provider, and TLS-provider worker identities are tracked explicitly; reentrant close skips only the current task/thread and attempts every noncurrent/later stage within the same original remaining budget; if the current frame owns counted bytes, a candidate/connection, or resource-stage state, close returns honestly in CLOSING with diagnostics.closed=false and those bytes still counted; the current finally epoch-rejects late results, wipes/releases exactly once, terminates its lane, and lets the coordinator publish CLOSED+zero and settle closeCompletion; if it owns no counted/resource-stage state, close may reach CLOSED synchronously; preserve current interrupt status and never resurrect a callback or resource
close.nonreturningCurrentCallback: a nonreturning current provider/task may keep state CLOSING and closeCompletion pending indefinitely with only the documented Wdns/Wcred/Wtls/Wop bounded daemon residual; diagnostics never report CLOSED or zero falsely

resourceFormulas.coreOperationDeadlineTasks: exactly one per admitted operation, capacity N
resourceFormulas.corePhaseTasks: at most one reusable connect/attempt/retry task per admitted operation, capacity N
resourceFormulas.engineTimerWheelTotalSlots: C+1, reactor-owned with no thread or core scheduler entry

concurrencyProtocol.completionRule: Release public in-flight and bytes before primary publication, publish outcome second for the same operation, release its reservation in finally, bound accepted terminal running-plus-queued work at exactly 2N, and promise no cross-operation ordering.
concurrencyProtocol.lockRule: Primitive mutable AdmissionState, WeightedByteBudget, and DiagnosticState use private short-held locks with global order AdmissionState -> WeightedByteBudget -> DiagnosticState; no reverse acquisition, global immutable RuntimeState CAS-copy, or lock across provider/network/parser/user callback.
concurrencyProtocol.lifecycleRule: First close linearizes OPEN to CLOSING and uses one original five-second monotonic deadline with exact scheduler -> relocated transport pool/connections/reactor including EngineTimerWheel -> DNS executor -> proxy credential executor -> TLS executor with owned session enumeration/invalidation submitted before shutdown -> operation executor -> terminal settlement pool -> observer dispatcher order; attempt all eight stages and emit at most one ordered close category per failed stage; reactor, operation, terminal, native-cancel, observer, DNS-provider, proxy-credential-provider, and TLS-provider reentrant close skips only current, preserves interrupt status, closes peers, and returns CLOSING with closed=false while current counted ownership remains; current finally epoch-rejects/wipes/releases, and only the coordinator may publish CLOSED at inFlight=0,bufferedBytes=0 and settle the one immutable closeCompletion stage; later close waits only the original remainder, and nonreturning current work leaves honest CLOSING with bounded residual; accepted terminal work is never discarded.
concurrencyProtocol.stressRule: Prove formulas at cpu/N boundaries, 65,536-operation timer and 131,072-settlement storms, H1/H2 cold bursts and settings changes, contention at 1/8/32 operation workers, 1,000 redeploy cycles, relocated-engine collectability, and zero owned thread/application-classloader retention.

cases.description: A custom Transport returning a null CompletionStage is one sanitized nonretryable IO/UNKNOWN/CUSTOM_TRANSPORT seam defect with no retry.
A non-null CompletionStage completing with null response is the shared no-response defect with exactly one transport invocation.
TransportResponse.of rejects invalid construction synchronously and closes a transferred non-null body exactly once before the throw escapes execute.
Asynchronous response construction failure is a failed stage; the transferred body is already closed exactly once and core never acquires a response.
Operation submission rejection releases admission and settles OVERLOADED/NOT_SENT through the bounded terminal pool.
Initial deadline registration rejection prevents operation work and settles OVERLOADED/NOT_SENT through the bounded terminal pool.
Retry timer rejection preserves unresolved commitment and settles OVERLOADED/POSSIBLY_SENT without a second attempt.
A borrowed executor running inline before submit returns performs no user or network work and terminalizes CONFIGURATION/NOT_SENT on the terminal pool.
Native Future cancellation removes the exact queued task when possible, prevents ghost work, and publishes the stable outcome once.
Runtime close cancels queued work by identity, uses structured CLOSED, and preserves borrowed executor ownership.
RepostFailureReason exposes only the closed shared enum/remediation mapping with JSpecify nullness and no platform exception strings.
Capture occurs at admission; operation/attempt scopes make the captured parent current around transport and owned continuations and every started handle ends once before publication.
A nonfatal capture failure disables telemetry for the operation, increments telemetryFailures once, and cannot change sending.
A nonfatal/null start disables telemetry, increments telemetryFailures once, and cannot change sending.
A nonfatal/null makeCurrent failure is isolated; retained handles receive best-effort end exactly once.
A nonfatal attempt start/end failure is isolated with no retry recursion and operation end still executes once.
A nonfatal end failure is counted once after the end attempt and cannot replace the primary terminal result.

A non-VM AssertionError receives allocation-minimal deterministic cleanup, completes primary and outcome exceptionally with the same instance, releases settlement in finally, and reaches an owned-thread uncaught handler as the same instance without retry or Repost normalization.

A non-VM LinkageError from a borrowed/JDK completion is handed off without user code, deterministically settles primary and outcome with the same instance and balanced resources, then is rethrown as that same instance on an owned terminal thread.
Nonfatal close failures aggregate in scheduler, transport, DNS, proxy credential, TLS, operation, terminal settlement, observer order while runtime still reaches CLOSED.
Verify every pinned upstream source archive, ordered minimal patch, patched tree, relocation, resource/service inventory, license, SBOM, provenance, and zero public or transitive upstream type.
Compile and execute the immutable HttpTransportOptions, proxy, DNS, TLS, borrowed ownership, mutual-exclusion, and non-overridable BUILTIN JSSE hostname-verification contract on JVM and native image.

Compile and execute the public ordered HeaderField-list ABI and prove its bounded one-pass iterator snapshots only a valid prefix, preserves valid duplicate order and case, never traverses an amplification tail, and prevents shuffled Map sources from creating implicit ordering.

Use latch-driven custom InputStreams with a pending read, cross-thread close, deadline, cancellation, runtime close, and saturated operation workers to prove close is prompt/callback-free, pending and future reads terminate, public terminal settlement progresses, and a later probe succeeds; separately bound the residual of an explicitly violating stream.
Prove exact connection and stream reservation acquisition/release boundaries, separate immutable body-chunk charging, diagnostics inclusion, and nonblocking overload without publication.
Drive cold bursts, ALPN single-flight, H1/H2 scale-out, peer stream limits 0/1/2/100/huge, FIFO cancellation, TTL, idle expiry, and GOAWAY replacement through the patched capacity-aware pool.
Cancel a queued stream before publication and terminalize active cancel, peer reset, parser, response-cap, and decompression failures while same-session peers and later reuse succeed.

Drive REFUSED_STREAM, CANCEL, and stream PROTOCOL_ERROR before and after commitment through success and exhaustion while raw-counting exact attempts, header/body publications, unresolved attempts, stable-key reuse, peer survival, and later same-session probe.

Queue N operations behind one cold establishment and prove only the oldest sponsor consumes a DNS/TCP/TLS failure, cancellation transfers useful establishment to the next waiter, zero waiters cancels it, and no failure fans out or creates publications.

Drive ordered dual-stack addresses through at most two complete raced TCP/CONNECT/TLS/ALPN pipelines for IPv6-blackhole to IPv4 success, first-TCP TLS stall or bad certificate with alternate healthy, reverse-family success, both fail, cancel, close, direct and proxy paths, shared deadlines, exact candidate slices, and loser cleanup.

Drive connect/attempt ties, connection readiness, retry backoff, cancellation, candidate cadence, expiry sweeps, and close to prove exact 2N core task reuse and a separate threadless C+1 reactor wheel.

Prove masked GOAWAY last-stream handling for local same-side streams, <=last retention, >last request-not-executed replay, queued-command cancellation, repeated GOAWAY, high reserved bit, peer isolation, and exact-once release.
Apply dynamic peer settings for zero-to-positive concurrency/window, reductions below active, unsigned huge values, and invalid frames without treating valid settings as connection-fatal.

Inspect raw HPACK while a huge peer table and rotating secret/idempotency/correlation/trace values prove secrets are NEVER_INDEXED, volatile values are WITHOUT_INDEXING, sensitivity survives relocation, and retained encoder bytes stay capped.

Exercise exact and plus-one raw/canonical base URI, resolved target, H1 request-line, local H1/H2 field-list, H2 encoded-block, and dynamic peer SETTINGS_MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE boundaries before stream or body publication.

Raw-capture zero, nonzero, and exact-maximum bodies on H1 and H2 and prove canonical actual-byte Content-Length, exact request/pseudo/regular field order and cardinality, explicit default-port authority preservation, local-cap accounting of automatic fields, and complete absence of Transfer-Encoding, chunked, duplicate Host/Content-Length, and connection-specific fields.
Exercise H1 and H2 eight-versus-nine informational responses and combined informational/final/trailer wire, logical, and field limits with H2 peer survival.

Reject H1 obs-fold, leading-name whitespace, TE plus CL, duplicate or invalid CL, unsupported/multiple TE, malformed chunks/extensions/trailers, and ambiguous framing with no connection reuse; reject forbidden H2 headers at stream scope.
Run H1/H2 multibyte boundaries plus malformed, overlong, truncated, control, DEL, and non-scalar header input through one REPORT-mode UTF-8 coding configuration and exact wire/logical accounting.

Block and saturate default and borrowed DNS resolution to prove caller/reactor isolation, deadline-before-submit, fixed-key bounded cache, exact rejection/removal, lifecycle ownership, and documented bounded noncooperative residual.

Use split-horizon fixture names to prove direct mode resolves only the origin, CONNECT mode resolves only the proxy, preserves canonical origin CONNECT authority and TLS SNI, and applies BUILTIN origin hostname verification without any origin-resolver call.

Block engine creation, configuration, session sizing, begin/status, wrap, unwrap, PKIX, OCSP, FIPS, HSM, verifier, and close calls to prove the full async TLS adapter preserves reactor and unrelated-session progress, deadline settlement, bounded residuals, per-connection serialization, and bounded performance cost.

Prove synchronous construction only validates syntax/allowlists, then first establishment lazily creates/probes each default non-global TLS context on the TLS lane under deadlines without ambient client keys; isolate trust/sessions/global-default mutation, invalidate owned sessions after connection close, and never mutate a borrowed context.

Block, cancel, fail, null, and saturate proxy credential providers to prove exact once-per-logical-establishment invocation on the credential pool, stable failure mapping, bounded candidate reuse, late-return discard, and wiping of every SDK-owned credential/CONNECT copy after all candidates settle.

Run exact username/password boundary and invalid matrices, raw-observe the preemptive Basic CONNECT field, rotate credentials across logical establishments, and prove invalid/null values publish zero CONNECT and every SDK-owned representation is wiped.

Drive raw proxy CONNECT responses through cumulative informational/header caps, strict non-2xx framing/body limits, slow-body deadline, cancellation, close, status tagging, credential removal, and poisoned-connection non-reuse; prove final 2xx immediately becomes a tunnel and preserves coalesced origin TLS octets despite bogus Content-Length/Transfer-Encoding.

Exercise exact and plus-one bodies and cancellation at every chunk boundary to prove bounded reactor copying, immutable chunks, no duplicate body, completion-after-raw-end, nonblocking InputStream, exact-once release, and protocol-local abort.

Inspect patched H1, H2, TLS, request-workspace, and raw/decompressed chunk storage after credential rotation, idle pool reuse, terminal cleanup, resize, and close; prove consumed/vacated SDK-owned plaintext is wiped while caller storage is untouched and wipe overhead stays bounded.

Construct every SDK JsonFactory with Jackson Core 2.21.2 nonRecyclingPool, disabled field-name canonicalization/interning, and exact stream constraints; parse unique unknown remote keys, then reuse one borrowed executor across isolated application-classloader redeploys and prove no recycler, symbol, key/payload sentinel, generated class, or application loader is retained.

Nearly exhaust the runtime close budget, jump wall time, and self-close from reactor, operation, native-cancel, terminal, and observer identities while proving one injected-monotonic five-second bound and interrupt preservation.

Invoke runtime.close reentrantly from DNS resolver, proxy credential provider, and custom JSSE provider callbacks while the current frame owns counted state; prove close returns honestly in CLOSING, skips only current, closes peers, preserves interrupt state, then current finally wipes/releases and completes the one close stage as CLOSED+zero; separately prove a nonreturning callback remains bounded and visibly CLOSING.

Prove the built-in engine ignores system properties and ambient proxy and disables upgrade, expect-continue, priority, redirects, retries, cookies, codecs, auth cache, target auth, server push, and hidden request replay.

Prove the Repost OTel bridge is authoritative across operation, attempts, reactor, DNS, TLS, retry, CompletionStage, and Kotlin coroutines while relocated internals remain agent-invisible and the only optional wire propagation is a fresh bridge-generated W3C traceparent per attempt.

concurrencyScenarios.description: JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-resource-formulas-cpu1-n1.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-resource-formulas-cpu4-n256.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-resource-formulas-cpu64-n65536.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-operation-worker-self-close.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-native-cancel-self-close.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-provider-worker-self-close.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-timer-storm-65536.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-core-timer-phase-reuse-and-ties.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-engine-timer-wheel-c-plus-one-close.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-terminal-storm-65536.
JVM deterministic concurrency proof for jvm-split-lock-contention-no-global-cas.
